reviews.review_title: Do not buy, if free save your time
reviews.review_description: The game started off with tremendous potential, but the state of the game right now is a rotting corpse. It’s dead and it’s been dead for quite some time now. Only a matter of time before it shuts down completely. Developers are no longer active for the game, with constant bugs glitches and cheaters plaguing the game. They made their money and are onto a new game called forefront which is actually worse. They got “new servers” which in reality means they down graded and you will lag constantly making the game unplayable. This game has ALWAYS had server issues made no mistake, but these new servers are atrocious. If you are on standalone using wifi, this game won’t work half the time. Please take my word as someone who used to love this game and has over 1.5k hrs in it, save your money and time and avoid this game at all costs.

reviews.review_title: Most cohesive and polished VR experience
reviews.review_description: I have been playing this game since it released on quest and I must say, this game has always been one of, if not the best games available on quest. Minimal bugs, good map and gun variety, and great graphics. Gameplay is easy to learn and hard to master, with simple controls and techniques to learn throughout your time playing the game. Even to this day I’m still learning new strategies in the game. Even with minimal content updates, this game is still very much alive with it’s strongest point being competitive mode. I seriously believe Breachers is the best option for a competitive VR game. With VR still being such a niche in gaming, its definitely a nice change of pace to have such a well structured game to enjoy. Overall, Breachers is the best VR game for casual, competitive, and even esports gamers.

Quite frankly, I don’t have a PC, not even a gaming computer, so I can’t play Siege, but from what I’ve seen, this game is a lot like Siege, and it just keeps getting better and better. I have a ton of fun playing this game. Yes, there are a lot of kids on here, but honestly, some of them are really good, and I hate to admit it, but they can carry you through games. So I would highly recommend this game to anyone, but you might want to be ready for some toxicity.

This game only comes back to life when it goes on sale. All the old players left or are at such a high skill level they dominate lobbies. The rank system is awful and it took over an hour just to find one lobby for Los Angeles! Looks like they outsourced their servers again to a worse company and game really fell off. I do not recommended buying this game.

reviews.review_title: Amazing potential but to many bugs
reviews.review_description: Amazing potential its my favourite game on vr but it has so many problems from matchmaking to bugs  and hitbox errors  it would be a great game if they fixed all theses issues but triangle factory havent and the game just dosent feel good to play alot of the time and it feels no way near as streamlined as a game like counter strike or rainbow six seige is.sometimes you have to wait for hours for a ranked game just then for it to be canceled due to one player not joining the game and then you queue again .however in other games this dosent happen and finding a ranked game only takes a minute or two .its a great game but theres to many bugs and problems with it.

Back in the early days of breachers where there wasn’t a meta, that was real fun. Devs keep adding crappy ai generated cosmetics and microtransactions to the shop and its virtually impossible to use any other weapons other than what is the current “meta”. Game loop designed to keep you playing and spending money rather than focused on the playerbase having fun, great idea for a vr game but turned to crap really quickly. Playerbase is full of sweaty chuds who allhave the same exact no life playstyle, casual players can’t have fun anymore because the comp players eat up whatever slop the devs churn out, they add useless gadgets to make the game feel „tactical“ but it never was in the slightest, everyone only uses the same two guns cuz they don’t bother actually balancing weapons so people who wanna try out new fun and creative loadouts never get a chance to have fun, you know the game is trash when the shotguns take more skill than the assault rifles. Game is so horrid it leaves you feeling disappointed even when you win, and the community is either little kids screaming, grown ahh men who are greasy losers who play like they have never seen a blade of grass in their lives, or toxic teammates that team kill you because they think its cool or edgy, its actually just annoying and exhausting, and imagine all of these „people“ in a singular lobby at once, it gets tiring. Devs left the game in the dirt and moved on to forefront, another port of a big game to vr just for some quick money before they move on to their next project after paying a bunch of youtubers to sponsor them and glaze their low effort game. Can’t play ranked, can’t play casual, yeah, I’m bad, but its not my fault every other person is just platinum 50 smurfing on a bronze account or hacking. Coming from a guy who has been playing since the alpha, I must say, this is my biggest spending regret of all time spending money on this game and if you are looking for real fun play a different game. Im not mad at the game and community I am just really disappointed at what we used to have and what could have been one of the greats of vr gaming.

reviews.review_title: Best tactical shooter in the vr market
reviews.review_description: This game is like if Seige and CS2 had a baby in the best way possible. I love the light destruction mechanics and how headshots are (mostly) one-shot. The community is also welcoming (for a tactical shooter) and funny. There are also many ways to attack/defend an objective. A lot of the time, I place explode a wall with a c4 on defense to get a better angle on unsuspecting attackers. The economy system also makes matches more interesting by opening up chances to try out different loadouts. I would say that you should probably have shooter game experience before playing this game. I recommend this game to any tactical shooter enjoyer

reviews.review_title: This game was good at one time..
reviews.review_description:
I’ve been playing this game since it was on sidequest and to be honest… it was literally the only game I actually ever really enjoyed playing. However based on multiple things; removing minor bugs yet leaving big exploits in the game like the vstock blind firing (which they fixed however people were crying about it that lead it being brought back to a more minimal degree), the servers are normally extremely bad and gets worse at peak times, and not actively patching things that need to be patch. The devs don’t listen to the community from what I’ve seen unless on something they already have plans to add in like comp skins for example

This game still has the potential of being great, however the devs need to start listening to the community like they once did before.

Everything about it is awesome. The Gadgets and their variety, the weapons, leaning, and game modes. Kills are satisfying and the destruction is great. 

 If more weapons are added, as well as unlockable attachments and gadgets, that would be awesome, As well as melee like knifes, hammers stuff like that. 

Buy this game, it’s so much fun.

reviews.review_title: I like the game but ranked is so trash
reviews.review_description: I love this game so much, but the ranked system is grueling. Every match is me getting put with bronzes and slivers while the other team is diamond master and gm or just before this review I got put into a match where in the FIRST 3 ROUNDS, 3 of my teammates dc so im forced to surrender and it sucks that I lost rank for that match for going 10 and 5 while the other teams bottom frag who went 0 and 5 gains rank. Honestly, ranked is the largest problem because why am losing rank for doing good and carrying my team. I think if ranked was based on the players' performance more than winning, the ranked system would be more fair and a way to fix missing 1 or more players leaving the match because 1 player gone, can tip the scale of the match heavily. In all this game is great with quick play and control point but ranked needs a major rework because it punishes the good players and rewards the bad players.

reviews.review_title: Good game worth the money but…
reviews.review_description: I have played this game for a while now and have found it to be very entertaining and fun to play, but my one downside I will say is the team matchmaking, how you get paired up with new players that have only played for some time, while the opposing team if full of the sweatiest people who are at max rank. Aside from that it’s a simple game, quick to understand, and big updates are very rare now that they’re working on forefront so keep that in mind. I will still say this is one of my top meta games that I own. Lately it has gotten worse for me as people just team kill, are toxic as can be, and just be as annoying as possible but that’s just on the players. The guns are actually very smooth and I find it satisfying to get a kill. Overall I think that this game I was good at one point and is still playable now. But maybe just wait for it to be on sale.

reviews.review_title: The rainbow six siege of VR!
reviews.review_description: Its so sweaty and impossible to play I don't recommend buying any triangle games, games  ever they are way to sweaty. At least its fun... kinda
This is my go-to game when I want to feel tactical and do breaches and stuff. It's awesome and I think everybody who has a VR headset should at least have this game.

Breachers is a 5v5 R6S inspired game with a CSGO weapon system. The game HAS an amazing foundation to work off of. The problem is the execution. 

Before I get into my gripes with the game I do want to highlight its strengths. The game does look visually appealing, like every Triangle Factory game they have their angular artstyle with great texture work and lighting. The gun modles are good and somewhat believable, and the game does have a nice array of gadgets to use which are unique to each team, defending and offensive. The game itself being $20 feels appropriate for what it is. The maps are wonderfully detailed and cool to look at and play on which is a rarity nowadays I feel. The soundscape is rich and has great fidelity, allowing for pinpointing a noise from far away or for judging distance and verticality just by sound. 

Now that I've got the pleasantries out of the way time to move on to what most people want to hear.

The biggest problem with the game is in fact the matching. You will be put in blatantly unfair lobbies against people who more than likely compete in tournaments regularly. You may have seen other reviews telling you not to worry about this as the game has a "high skill ceiling", and the people who bring up this up just have a "skill issue". Think what you want to think, but the truth is the matchmaking is against you. When people say they are getting put up against diamond 2's while they're a bronze is not an overstatement, it is literally what happens. You will lose many games solely to this problem, and you may not have fun just because of it. So take out of that what you will. 

Next issue is the gun balance. I don't know why people haven't brought up this issue more, because it is bad. Every gun in the game besides the: Taurus, Vesin, and Spectral, are almost just gimmicks. Every other gun in the game doesn't do enough damage to rationalise using it, or it just shoots so slow you'd be better off using it as a club. By the time people start buying Vesin's the game is over. The Vesin if you don't know, is a fairly priced, high rate of fire, high damage, highly modable, low recoil assault rifle, with a good magazine size. So you'd think there'd be no reason to use any other gun right? Well your right 86% of people do! The only time someone uses a different gun is if they lack the funds, or are memeing.

Another problem which seems weird to bring up is the movement, not saying that it's bad, but the people abuse it to its limit. The high rank players are the worst offenders being, because of their use of crouch spamming and hyper jiggle peaking making them honestly really hard to hit. Which you COULD say is a gamplay element, but just because it's in the game doesnt mean its fun to play against. Which people don't seem to understand. 

The game doesn't seem to inspire or encourage strategy. In my experience nobody really tries to use some sort of play or use gadgets to their full potential, most of the time it feels like I'm playing a team death match with a bomb in middle of the map. The most you'll see is maybe a drone, some trip wires, and the cloak especially, but not much else unless someone really likes setting defenses. 

All in all the game is good for a competitive tactical shooter, but it's drawbacks hinder it's fun value. If you want the game get it, but if your having second thoughts after reading this then maybe trust your gut on this one. Also the game has an in-game voice chat monitor so you can't swear unless you want a voice chat ban, so if you like saying slurs you're out of luck here. That's all I have to say.
Also Devs = losers
